req.title: System-Admin updates policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a policy-document through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path update by an authenticated admin (EP – valid partition), (2) missing mandatory field triggering 400 (NEG / DT – invalid input decision), (3) unauthenticated caller triggering 401 (NEG), (4) forbidden role triggering 403 (DT), (5) title at exactly maximum allowed length (BVA), and (6) state transition from 'draft' to 'published' (STC). Together these satisfy the minimum positive/negative/boundary requirement and give broad coverage of the error pathways and audit obligation stated in R-WEB-054.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_policy_document_success
preconditions: A policy-document with a known ID exists
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
Capture response status and body
Query audit log via <get_latest_audit_record> for the document ID
expected: Response body contains the entity id
Audit record exists for the update action
trace: R-WEB-054: Expected outcomes – policy-document is updated; audit record written; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_admin_updates_policy_document_success(
    admin_token,        # <fixture> returns valid admin JWT
    existing_doc_id,    # <fixture> creates and yields a policy-document id
    get_latest_audit_record,  # <helper_fn>
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    payload = {
        "title": "Updated Policy Title",
        "content": "Updated content body.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{existing_doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response body missing entity id"
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=existing_doc_id)
    assert audit is not None, "No audit record written"
    assert audit["action"] == "update"

tests.name: test_update_policy_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: A policy-document with a known ID exists
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} omitting the mandatory 'title' field
Capture response status and error body
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'title'
trace: R-WEB-054: Error pathways – 400 on validation error
R-WEB-054: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_update_policy_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    admin_token,       # <fixture>
    existing_doc_id,   # <fixture>
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    payload = {
        # 'title' intentionally omitted
        "content": "Some content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{existing_doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "title" in error_text, "Validation error did not reference missing field 'title'"

tests.name: test_update_policy_document_title_at_max_length
preconditions: A policy-document with a known ID exists
Maximum allowed title length is 255 characters
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Construct a title string of exactly 255 characters
Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with that title
Capture response status
expected: Response body contains the entity id
trace: R-WEB-054: Mandatory fields must be validated (boundary at max length)
R-WEB-054: Expected outcomes – policy-document is updated; response 200 or 201
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_update_policy_document_title_at_max_length(
    admin_token,       # <fixture>
    existing_doc_id,   # <fixture>
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    assert len(max_title) == MAX_TITLE_LEN
    payload = {
        "title": max_title,
        "content": "Boundary test content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-06-01"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{existing_doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_update_policy_document_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: A policy-document with a known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} without an Authorization header
Capture response status
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_update_policy_document_unauthenticated_returns_401(
    existing_doc_id,   # <fixture>
):
    payload = {
        "title": "No Auth Update",
        "content": "Content without auth.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{existing_doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        # No Authorization header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_update_policy_document_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'viewer' (not admin)
A policy-document with a known ID exists
actions: Obtain an auth token for a viewer-role user via <get_viewer_token>
Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with a valid payload
Capture response status
trace: R-WEB-054: Error pathways – 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_update_policy_document_draft_to_published_state_transition
preconditions: A policy-document with status 'draft' and a known ID exists
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with status set to 'published'
Capture response status and body
Re-fetch document via GET /api/policy-documents/{id} to verify persisted state
trace: R-WEB-054: Expected outcomes – policy-document is updated
R-WEB-054: State transition draft -> published covered by STC heuristic
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_update_policy_document_draft_to_published_state_transition(
    admin_token,          # <fixture>
    draft_doc_id,         # <fixture> creates a doc with status='draft', yields id
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    payload = {
        "title": "Policy v1",
        "content": "Final approved content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-03-01",
        "status": "published"
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{draft_doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert put_resp.json().get("status") == "published"
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/policy-documents/{draft_doc_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "published"
